behavior_summary: RAT::look: When the player 'looks at' (inspects) the rat NPC, the rat emits a short textual/audible bark containing the literal string "skellot ". This is purely a descriptive/UX response to inspection.

RAT::gotHit: When the rat receives a hit (damage) the handler first checks internal flags and whether the NPC is dead. If alive and conditions pass, it iterates over associated items (likely inventory/contained items) and for qualifying items (weight > 3 or a per-item flag) plays a specific SFX and forwards a small hit/effect to each item via Item::receiveHit. The routine propagates part of the hit effect to carried/contained objects and emits feedback.
notable_control_flow: RAT::look is an Event 0 handler that performs a single, deterministic action (Item::bark) then returns — no branching based on game state observed.
RAT::gotHit is guarded: it returns early if a specific rat/object flag test fails or if Npc::isDead() is true; only otherwise does it enter a loop over items to apply further effects and sounds.
The gotHit loop applies per-item guard checks (weight and/or a flag) before invoking playSFX and Item::receiveHit, so item propagation is selective, not universal.
open_questions: What exact flag (the checked rat/object field compared with 0x0100) represents (defensive stance, immunity, or a scripted state)?
What is the full meaning of the per-item flag tested in the loop (what attribute causes an item to be skipped besides weight)?
Which game events or AI states invoke RAT::gotHit (exact event index inferred as 6 but game mapping to engine events is not present here)?

main_function.name: U8P_RAT::look
main_function.purpose: Provide the player-facing inspection response for the rat NPC by emitting a short descriptive/audible string when looked at.
entry_conditions: Event 0 — invoked when the Avatar/player 'looks at' or inspects this rat NPC

function_map.name: U8P_RAT::look
function_map.role: Inspection/description handler — barks a literal string to the viewer

function_map.name: U8P_RAT::gotHit
function_map.role: Damage/hit handler — guards against dead state, iterates associated items and forwards hit/effects to qualifying items and plays SFX

function_map.name: Item::bark
function_map.role: Emit a short UI/audible message or description from an item/NPC

function_map.name: Npc::isDead
function_map.role: Query whether an NPC is dead — used as an early exit guard in gotHit

function_map.name: Item::getWeightIncludingContents
function_map.role: Return the weight of an item including contents — used to decide propagation of hit

function_map.name: U8P_RAT::playSFX
function_map.role: Play a sound effect (used for feedback during hit propagation)

function_map.name: Item::receiveHit
function_map.role: Apply a hit/effect to an item instance — used to forward damage/effects to carried/contained objects

external_calls.purpose: Show/emit the literal inspection string "skellot " to the player/viewer
Check if the rat is already dead and abort further processing if so
Obtain each associated item's weight (including contents) to decide whether to apply propagated hit/effects
Emit an audible cue (SFX id 0x55) when an item qualifies for hit propagation
Forward/apply a small hit/effect to qualifying carried/contained items (propagation of the rat's hit to its items)

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: RAT::look: When the player 'looks at' (inspects) the rat NPC, the rat emits a short textual/audible bark containing the literal string "skellot ". This is purely a descriptive/UX response to inspection.
